__inputs_doc__: Datasource UIDs below match docker/config/grafana-datasources.yml provisioned UIDs (mimir, tempo, loki). For manual Grafana Import, run scripts/sanitize-dashboards.sh to convert UIDs to __inputs template variables.
__tier_doc__: OSS overview. Every Prometheus query here resolves against a series populated on the OSS @baldur.protected path (Circuit Breaker, Retry Outcomes) or the Django HTTP metrics middleware (latency). PRO-only series (DLQ, throttle, canary, emergency, notification, watchdog, bulkhead) live in baldur-operations.json so an OSS-only stack renders no mystery-empty panels. The Retry Outcomes panel populates from the inline synchronous @baldur.protected(retry=True) path: every terminal retry outcome (success / exhausted / single-attempt failure) records to baldur_retry_outcomes_total, so it is live on any protected retry traffic — not only the Celery/DLQ retry paths.
title: Baldur Overview (OSS)
description: OSS-tier observability reference: Circuit Breaker state, HTTP latency, recent error traces, Retry outcomes, Idempotency gate decisions, Health Check status, System Control kill-switch, and Graceful Shutdown. All series populate on OSS paths with no PRO tier.

panels.title: Circuit Breaker State
panels.description: Each service's circuit breaker: CLOSED = healthy (calls pass through), OPEN = tripped (calls fast-fail to protect a failing dependency), HALF-OPEN = probing recovery. An OPEN breaker means Baldur is actively shielding that dependency.

panels.title: HTTP Latency P95 / P99
panels.description: Per-request HTTP latency at the 95th and 99th percentiles, in milliseconds. Rising P95/P99 means your slowest requests are getting slower — read it against your latency SLO. Empty for a plain-Python service with no HTTP surface.

panels.title: Recent Traces (Errors)
panels.description: The most recent error-status request traces. Click a row to open the full span timeline in Tempo and see where the request failed.

panels.title: Retry Outcomes
panels.description: Rate of retry outcomes: success (a retry eventually worked), exhausted (every retry failed and the error surfaced), single-attempt failure (failed with no retry). Rising 'exhausted' means retries are no longer absorbing the failures.

panels.title: Idempotency Gate Decisions
panels.description: Rate of idempotency-gate decisions: continue (first time — run the work), skip (already done — deduplicated), abort (in flight elsewhere). Rising skip/abort means the gate is catching duplicate work before it runs twice.

panels.title: Health Check Status & Results
panels.description: Aggregate health per check: 0 = healthy, 1 = degraded, 2 = unhealthy, 3 = error — plus the rate of check results. Anything above 0 means a dependency check is failing.

panels.title: System Control (Kill Switch)
panels.description: The global System Control kill switch: 1 = enabled, 0 = disabled, plus the cumulative number of times it has been toggled.

panels.title: Graceful Shutdown
panels.description: Shutdown phase (0 = running, 1 = draining, 2 = terminating, 3 = terminated) plus how many in-flight requests were drained vs. force-aborted on shutdown. Sits at 0/running normally and moves only during a SIGTERM drain; the drained/aborted counts tell you whether a restart lost in-flight work.
